core_ideas: 1) In a collision between two objects, the objects have to come into contact; sometimes something is damaged, but not always.
Different factors and variables may cause objects to be damaged or not damaged in a collision.
misconceptions: a) If nothing looks damaged after the collision, the force must have
b) Heavier objects always cause more damage. (not thinking about other

quiz_items.stem: A phone drops onto a soft pillow. The phone looks the same afterward. Which statement best describes the collision?

options.A: The force was weak because nothing was damaged.
options.B: The phone and pillow pushed on each other.
options.D: Only the phone pushed; the pillow did not.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?
A bug hits a car windshield while the car is moving on the highway. The windshield looks the same afterward. Which statement best describes the collision?

options.A: The force must have been weak because the windshield was not damaged.
options.B: The bug and the windshield pushed on each other during contact.
options.C: The windshield exerted a force, but the bug did not because it is small.
options.D: There was no collision because the windshield did not change shape.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?

A heavy bowling ball hits the same window very slowly. A light golf ball hits a window very fast. The bowling ball does break the window, but the golf ball broke the window. Which statement best explain it?

options.A: Heavier objects always cause more damage.
options.B: Speed can matter as well as mass.
options.C: The window must have been defective.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?
A large truck and a small compact car collide head on. The car is badly crumpled, but the truck shows much less damage. Which statement best explains why the damage differs?

options.A: The truck must have exerted a much larger force to the car.
options.B: Damage depends on factors such as materials and design of each object, not only mass.
options.C: The car was damaged because it exerted no force on the truck.
options.D: The truck was less damaged because smaller objects have less gravity.

quiz_items.stem: How confident are you about your answer?
